dublinCore.dc:title: 114 S4815 IS: Communities Helping Invest through Property and Improvements Needed for Veterans Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mrs. Fischer (for herself and Mr. Ricketts) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Veterans’ Affairs

form.official-title: To permanently extend the pilot program of the Department of Veterans Affairs on acceptance by the Department of donated facilities and related improvements and to authorize acceptance of donations of construction services, minor construction or nonrecurring maintenance projects, and targeted contributions, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Communities Helping Invest through Property and Improvements Needed for Veterans Act of 2026 or the CHIP IN for Veterans Act of 2026.

section.header: Permanent extension of pilot program on acceptance by the Department of Veterans affairs of donated facilities and related improvements

subsection.text: Section 2 of the Communities Helping Invest through Property and Improvements Needed for Veterans Act of 2016 (Public Law 114–294; 38 U.S.C. 8103 note) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the section heading, by striking Pilot;
in subsection (a), in the subsection heading, by striking Pilot;
by striking pilot each place it appears;

subsection.text: Paragraph (1) of subsection (b) of such section is amended to read as follows:

subparagraph.text: a need identified in a Strategic Capital Investment Planning process priority list, a five-year development plan, a facility master plan, or an annual capital needs inventory of the Department; or
any component or phase of a need described in paragraph (1); and

section.header: Authority to accept donations of construction services, minor construction or nonrecurring maintenance projects, and targeted contributions

subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs may accept donations comprising the total cost or a portion of the cost of—

subparagraph.text: to an existing facility of the Department; or
to a new facility or portion thereof of the Department.

subsection.text: The Secretary may accept a donation under this section only if—

subparagraph.text: a need identified in a Strategic Capital Investment Planning process priority list, a five-year development plan, a facility master plan, or an annual capital needs inventory of the Department; or
any component or phase of a need described in subparagraph (A);

paragraph.text: the donation is from an entity described in section 2(a)(2) of the Communities Helping Invest through Property and Improvements Needed for Veterans Act of 2016 (Public Law 114–294; 38 U.S.C. 8103 note);
the Secretary determines such donation would—

subparagraph.text: reduce the expense to the Department;

paragraph.text: the donor enters into a formal agreement with the Secretary that includes—

subparagraph.text: provisions for the Department’s oversight during performance;
compliance with applicable construction codes and standards, and applicable laws and regulations;
donor-provided insurance, warranties, and liability protections;
the amount of the donation and the amount of the Department’s funding contribution, if any;
that the donation shall not increase the cost to the Federal Government of completing such project described in subsection (a) (excluding activation and sustainment of such facility); and
such other terms as the Secretary determines necessary.

subsection.text: For donations under this section that do not involve transfer of real property title—

paragraph.text: the donor shall enter into an agreement with the Department that determines who is responsible to ensure environmental or historic preservation due diligence is completed;
the donor shall obtain all federally required construction and facility related permits; and

agreements may be simplified relative to those under section 2 of the Communities Helping Invest through Property and Improvements Needed for Veterans Act of 2016 (Public Law 114–294; 38 U.S.C. 8103 note) to reflect the nature of services or targeted contributions.

subsection.text: The Secretary shall include information on donations accepted under this section in the reports required under section 2(g) of the Communities Helping Invest through Property and Improvements Needed for Veterans Act of 2016 (Public Law 114–294; 38 U.S.C. 8103 note), with separate tracking for donations under this section.
